title: M3: TLS/TCPS + ewallet.pem + tnsnames/IFILE + connect-string corpus differential (anti-fake-parity); cwallet.sso value-add

M5: Arrow dataframes (arrow-rs RecordBatch + C Data Interface shim export) + direct path load (TTC 128/129/130) — modules 8000-9400,9600,9700 match-or-beat baseline; upstream-WIP bugs fixed not reproduced (Q7)
M6: Gauntlet certification — filtered 72-module suite matches-or-beats baseline, fuzz clean, fake-parity sweep, perf vs python-oracledb+rust-oracle incl. bulk-load
M4: DbObject/XMLType + OSON/JSON + VECTOR + pool/DRCP + pipelining + sessionless txn + all async modules
M2: execute/fetch/binds + core scalar types + LOB/LONG — suite 1000-5200 series matches baseline
notes:
M2 checkpoint: advanced the connection-suite baseline from 50 passed / 5 failed / 7 skipped to 55 passed / 7 skipped for reference/python-oracledb/tests/test_1100_connection.py under the Rust pyshim.

Implemented this slice:
- ThinPoolImpl now has stateful constructor/config accessors, close/reconfigure/setters, and open-state errors sufficient for test_1134. Real connection pooling and acquire/return semantics remain future work.
- EndUserSecurityContextImpl now stores parsed fields, enforces the >65535 length guard, exposes clear_end_user_security_context, and raises the expected non-TCPS error path for set_end_user_security_context. Real EUS OSON/piggyback wire support remains M4/future work.
- Added call-timeout support in the Rust crate with timeout-aware blocking execute wrappers and CallTimeout mapping to DPY-4024.
- Released the Python GIL around cursor mutex acquisition and blocking Rust network I/O, which fixed the thread/cancel deadlock exposed by test_1125.
- Added a pyshim cancel flag path that makes public cancel semantics pass locally; the lower-level marker cancel primitive remains experimental and is not used by the shim yet because the local listener left protocol state inconsistent after marker use.
- Local test DB setup required a SYSDBA grant of execute on sys.dbms_lock to pythontest so DBMS_LOCK sleep tests exercise the intended paths.

Current gates completed: cargo fmt --all --check; cargo test -p oracledb-protocol -p oracledb -p oracledb-pyshim; maturin develop; focused live pytest for 1125/1126/1131 and 1134/1160/1161; full live test_1100_connection.py with 55 passed / 7 skipped; ubs crates/oracledb/src/lib.rs crates/oracledb-pyshim/src/lib.rs harness/shim_inject/__init__.py exited 0 with 0 critical issues and warning-level inventory only; git diff --check passed. M2 remains in_progress because the broader 1000-5200 module parity target is not complete.

title: M1: Connect + 12c/11g auth + identity masquerade gate (v$session reflects caller-set osuser/program/machine) + test_1100 green
notes: M1 progress: Rust thin CONNECT/ACCEPT, fast auth phase one/two, 12c verifier, marker reset, server piggyback skip, ping, close are live-green against localhost:1522/FREEPDB1. PyO3 ThinConnImpl now bridges public oracledb.connect() to Rust for connect attributes, ping, close using harness env password. Identity gate verified via reference query: program/machine/terminal/osuser/client_driver matched Rust-set values. Remaining M1 gate blocker: python-oracledb test_1100 setup calls conn.cursor() for env initialization; ThinConnImpl.create_cursor_impl is still an explicit placeholder, so execute/fetch cursor implementation is next.
close_reason: Completed M1: Rust thin auth/connect, execute/fetch text cursor, identity gates test_1146-test_1150, and python-oracledb test_1100 pass through PyO3 shim

close_reason: Completed M0 harness foundation: pinned python-oracledb v4.0.1, local Oracle Free harness, green 72-module baseline manifest, Rust PyO3 shim manifest with honest NotImplemented failures, workspace/asupersync scaffold, cargo gates clean
